label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to work through this question. The question is asking why weekend afternoons would become dead time without sports programs. First, I need to recall the information given in the passage. The passage states that sports events are the most popular weekend programs, especially among male viewers who might not watch much TV at other times. It also mentions that Saturday and Sunday afternoons are the slowest time periods for general TV viewing. So, without sports, those time slots would not attract as many viewers. The passage also explains that because sports draw in a large audience during these times, TV networks can sell advertising time at higher prices. If there were no sports, the TV networks wouldn't have the same audience, so the time slots wouldn't be as valuable. The term "dead time" here refers to low viewership, which would make those time slots less desirable for advertisers. Looking at the options: A) Because there would be few viewers. This aligns with the idea that without sports, the time slots would have low ratings, which the passage mentions as "dead time." B) Advertisers off work? The passage doesn't mention anything about advertisers' work schedules. It's more about the audience and their value. C) Programs would go slowly. The passage doesn't talk about the pace of the programs, but rather the viewership numbers. D) Viewers would pay less. The passage doesn't discuss viewers' payment habits but rather the value of advertising
